anxiety_tips.app_subtitle: Simple techniques to help you feel grounded and safe.
anxiety_tips.support_footer: If anxiety feels overwhelming or persistent, consider speaking with a mental health professional.
anxiety_tips.tip_seek_support_desc: Talk to someone you trust to gain reassurance and perspective.
anxiety_tips.tip_deep_breathing_desc: Slow breathing can calm your nervous system quickly.
anxiety_tips.tip_mindfulness_desc: Focus on the present moment without judgment.
anxiety_tips.tip_muscle_relaxation_desc: Release physical tension to ease anxiety.
anxiety_tips.tip_positive_self_talk_desc: Replace anxious thoughts with realistic, calming statements.
anxiety_tips.breathing_why: Anxiety activates the fight-or-flight response. Slow breathing signals safety to your brain and body.
anxiety_tips.breathing_step1: Inhale through your nose for 4 seconds
anxiety_tips.mindfulness_why: Anxiety often focuses on future fears. Mindfulness brings your attention back to the present moment.
anxiety_tips.muscle_why: Anxiety creates physical tension. Releasing muscles sends calming signals to your brain.
anxiety_tips.selftalk_why: Anxious thoughts often exaggerate danger. Balanced thinking reduces fear intensity.
anxiety_tips.selftalk_step4: Replace it with a realistic statement
anxiety_tips.support_why: Anxiety can make worries feel bigger than they are. Sharing them with someone trusted can reduce isolation and provide reassurance.
